title: Exercise Nutrition Timing — Paper Catalogue
summary: Exercise Nutrition Timing — Paper Catalogue Ingested: 2026-04-25 Source: PubMed systematic searches (2017–2026), terms: pre/during/post exercise nutrition, carbohydrate timing, nutrient timing, intermittent sports nutrition, glycogen resynthesis, protein synthesis, team sports fuelling. Synthesised into: wiki/general/nutrition.md --- ## Paper 1 — Kerksick et al. 2017 ⭐ PRIMARY POSITION STAND Title: International Society of Sports Nutrition Position Stand: Nutrient Timing Authors: Kerksick CM, Arent S, Schoenfeld BJ, Stout JR, Campbell B, Wilborn CD, Taylor L, Kalman D, Smith-Ryan AE, Kreider RB, Willoughby D, Arciero PJ, VanDusseldorp TA, Ormsbee MJ, Wildman R, Greenwood M, Ziegenfuss TN, Aragon AA, Antonio J Journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition Year: 2017 (Aug 29) PMID: 28919842 PMCID: PMC5596471 DOI: 10.1186/s12970-017-0189-4 Access: Free PMC full text Type: Position Stand / Expert Review (ISSN) Key findings: - Glycogen maximised at 8–12 g/kg/day dietary carbohydrate - If <4h between sessions: 1.2 g/kg/hr CHO with high-GI sources; adding caffeine (3–8 mg/kg) further accelerates resynthesis - During exercise >70% VO2max: 30–60 g CHO/hr in 6–8% solution every 10–15 min for efforts >70 min - Post-exercise (0–2h window): 20–40 g high-quality protein stimulates robust MPS increases - Protein dose every 3–4 hours (0.25–0.40 g/kg/dose) optimises MPS throughout day - Casein 30–40 g pre-sleep acutely increases overnight MPS without impairing lipolysis - Combining CHO + protein (0.8 + 0.2–0.4 g/kg/hr) during recovery reduces total CHO requirement while maintaining glycogen resynthesis --- ## Paper 2 — Bonilla et al.

2017 ⭐ PRIMARY POSITION STAND Title: International Society of Sports Nutrition Position Stand: Nutrient Timing Authors: Kerksick CM, Arent S, Schoenfeld BJ, Stout JR, Campbell B, Wilborn CD, Taylor L, Kalman D, Smith-Ryan AE, Kreider RB, Willoughby D, Arciero PJ, VanDusseldorp TA, Ormsbee MJ, Wildman R, Greenwood M, Ziegenfuss TN, Aragon AA, Antonio J Journal: Journal of the International Society of Sports Nutrition Year: 2017 (Aug 29) PMID: 28919842 PMCID: PMC5596471 DOI: 10.1186/s12970-017-0189-4 Access: Free PMC full text Type: Position Stand / Expert Review (ISSN) Key findings: - Glycogen maximised at 8–12 g/kg/day dietary carbohydrate - If <4h between sessions: 1.2 g/kg/hr CHO with high-GI sources; adding caffeine (3–8 mg/kg) further accelerates resynthesis - During exercise >70% VO2max: 30–60 g CHO/hr in 6–8% solution every 10–15 min for efforts >70 min - Post-exercise (0–2h window): 20–40 g high-quality protein stimulates robust MPS increases - Protein dose every 3–4 hours (0.25–0.40 g/kg/dose) optimises MPS throughout day - Casein 30–40 g pre-sleep acutely increases overnight MPS without impairing lipolysis - Combining CHO + protein (0.8 + 0.2–0.4 g/kg/hr) during recovery reduces total CHO requirement while maintaining glycogen resynthesis --- ## Paper 2 — Bonilla et al.

2020 ⭐ 4R FRAMEWORK Title: The 4R's Framework of Nutritional Strategies for Post-Exercise Recovery: A Review with Emphasis on New Generation of Carbohydrates Authors: Bonilla DA, Pérez-Idárraga A, Odriozola-Martínez A, Kreider RB Journal: International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health Year: 2020 (Dec 24) PMID: 33375691 DOI: 10.3390/ijerph18010103 Access: Free PMC Type: Review The 4R Framework: 1.
